replication_study.hypothesis: In ESS rounds around 2010–2011 (replication dataset), individuals with higher immigration concern (imm_concern) will report higher political distrust in their national parliament (trstprl_rev), controlling for individual- and country-level covariates.

data_plan.dataset_identifier: ESS replication dataset (circa 2010–2011), cleaned RDS files: data_clean.rds, data_clean_5pct.rds; imputed data: data_imp_5pct.rds
data_plan.source_type: Cross-national survey (European Social Survey) with merged country-level indicators (e.g., WGI, GDP per capita, social expenditure, far-right vote share).
data_plan.wave_or_subset: ESS circa 2010–2011 (post-2009, distinct from original ESS Rounds 1–4). Countries observed in replication sample include DE, GR, IE, GB, PT, FI, ES, NL, FR, BE, DK, SE, NO, CH.
data_plan.sample_size: Full cleaned dataset: 25,586 rows (data_clean.rds). 5% sample: 1,279 rows (data_clean_5pct.rds). Complete-case analysis in code results in ~858 observations (weights model) due to missingness.
data_plan.unit_of_analysis: Individual respondents (nested within countries; country-year present via 'year').
data_plan.access_details: All replication RDS files and R code are provided in '/app/data' at runtime. No external access required.

qualification.explanation: This dataset is well-suited for replication because it uses the same survey source (ESS), constructs, and covariate structure as the original study while providing a different temporal sample (post-2009). This permits testing whether the positive association between immigration concern and political distrust in parliament generalizes to a new time period and sample of countries.
qualification.similarity_to_original: Original data: ESS rounds 1–4 (2002–2009) with reversed trust scales and a multi-item immigration concern measure, and country-level covariates including governance quality and far-right party metrics (post_registration.json; original_paper.pdf summary). Replication data: Contains trstprl_rev (reverse-coded trust in parliament), imm_concern (from reversed items imbgeco_rev, imueclt_rev, imwbcnt_rev), weights (pspwght), and country-level variables (wgi, gdppc, socexp, vote_share_fr, lt_imm_cntry) matching the constructs in the original (see columns in data_clean.rds and data_clean_5pct.rds; functions.get_dataset_columns/info observations). Thus, the measurement strategy closely mirrors the original.
qualification.deviation_from_original: Original sample period: 2002–2009 (ESS Rounds 1–4) per post_registration.json and original_paper.pdf. Replication sample period: 2010–2011 (year variable summary in data_clean.rds/data_clean_5pct.rds). Original analysis spanned 16 countries and specified three-level HLM (individuals nested in country-rounds nested in countries; post_registration.json). Replication data include 14 Western European countries (cntry categories list from data_clean.rds) and the provided code estimates a two-level random-intercept model by country (data_analysis_code.R). This constitutes an independent sample and slightly simplified nesting, while preserving key measures.

data_plan.notes: Variables are present with expected names and coding (e.g., trstprl_rev, imm_concern, pspwght, wgi, gdppc, socexp, vote_share_fr, lt_imm_cntry). Some variables are objects representing factors (e.g., vote_gov, vote_frparty) and are treated as factors by lmer. Data show substantial missingness for several controls, explaining reduced N in complete-case runs. The imputed dataset (data_imp_5pct.rds) is a mice 'mids' object; our dataset tools cannot parse it, but R code handles it correctly. Year coverage is 2010–2011. All file I/O must use /app/data at runtime; code has been patched to do so.

planned_method.steps: 1) Load data_clean_5pct.rds from /app/data for a complete-case weighted multilevel model (primary quick replication as per provided script). 2) Optionally, run the unweighted complete-case model for robustness. 3) Load data_imp_5pct.rds (mids) and estimate the imputed-data models with weights and without weights; pool estimates using mice::pool. 4) (Optional extension) Re-run the weighted model on the full dataset (data_clean.rds) to increase power and assess stability. 5) Compare the imm_concern coefficient (sign, magnitude, significance) to the original paper’s estimate for distrust in parliament. 6) Document any deviations (sample period/countries, nesting structure) and interpret robustness.
planned_method.models: Linear mixed-effects models (lmer) with random intercepts for country: trstprl_rev ~ imm_concern + controls + (1 | cntry); with and without sampling weights (pspwght). Imputed data models estimated with mice::with and pooled via mice::pool.
planned_method.outcome_variable: trstprl_rev (reverse-coded trust in parliament; higher values indicate more distrust).
planned_method.independent_variables: Primary: imm_concern. Controls: happy_rev, stflife_rev, sclmeet_rev, distrust_soc, stfeco_rev, hincfel, stfhlth_rev, stfedu_rev, vote_gov, vote_frparty, lrscale, hhinc_std, agea, educ, female, vote_share_fr, socexp, lt_imm_cntry, wgi, gdppc, unemp.
planned_method.control_variables: As listed in independent_variables above; these match the core set in the original design (post_registration.json).
planned_method.tools_software: R (lme4 for multilevel modeling; mice for multiple imputation and pooling).

planned_estimation_and_test.estimation: Coefficient of imm_concern in the lmer model for trstprl_rev (both complete-case and imputed-data pooled estimates).
planned_estimation_and_test.test: t-statistics from lmer (Satterthwaite-like approximations not used unless lmerTest is added). Pooling with mice uses Rubin’s rules; two-sided tests with alpha=0.05, assessing positive direction consistency.

planned_method.missing_data_handling: Primary: multiple imputation via mice (as supplied in data_imp_5pct.rds) with pooled estimates; Secondary: complete-case analysis as presented in the script.
planned_method.multiple_testing_policy: Single primary outcome (distrust in parliament). If additional outcomes are analyzed, control the false discovery rate (Benjamini–Hochberg) across outcomes.
planned_method.inference_criteria: Replicate if the imm_concern coefficient is positive and statistically significant at p<0.05 in the weighted model; compare point estimate magnitude to original (target ~0.17) for directional and approximate magnitude consistency.

files.data_analysis_code.R: Main R script that estimates the multilevel models: (1) weighted complete-case model on data_clean_5pct.rds; (2) unweighted complete-case model; (3) weighted imputed-data model on data_imp_5pct.rds with mice::with and mice::pool; (4) unweighted imputed-data model. It prints summaries to the console and does not write output files. Paths have been updated to read from /app/data.

codebase.notes: Dependencies: R packages lme4 and mice. Script assumes data files exist in /app/data. It currently models random intercepts by country; this is a simplification relative to the original three-level model but suffices for the focal coefficient replication. No external APIs or additional scripts required.

analysis.instructions: 1) Place data_clean_5pct.rds, data_imp_5pct.rds (and optionally data_clean.rds) into ./data so they appear at /app/data in the container. 2) Run: Rscript /path/to/data_analysis_code.R. 3) Inspect console output for the imm_concern coefficient in the weighted complete-case model (m1) and the pooled imputed model (fitimp1). 4) (Optional) Modify the script to use data_clean.rds to run the full-sample model and compare estimates. 5) Record sign, magnitude, and p-value of imm_concern and compare with original results.
analysis.comparison_metrics: Direction (positive), statistical significance (p<0.05), and coefficient magnitude vs. original parliament estimate (~0.17 per post_registration.json and original_paper.pdf summary). Optionally compute percent difference from original and whether confidence intervals overlap if available.
